items.headline: City addresses affordability protections for subsidized housing projects
items.summary: Council considered new policies on City Affordability Restrictions for project-based subsidies, addressing what happens to affordable housing when subsidies expire or properties face foreclosure. This directly impacts long-term affordability of assisted housing units across San José.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 construction underway on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four new homes permitted on Altino Boulevard as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development, with prices ranging from $487K to $573K. This represents significant residential infill in the area with multiple units approved simultaneously.

items.headline: Sherly Place development adds three new homes to neighborhood
items.summary: Three new homes permitted at Sherly Place with a 100% BEMP (Build Equity Model Program) designation, priced between $496K-$570K. The affordable housing focus makes this notable for increasing homeownership opportunities in San José.

items.headline: City Council reviews capital improvement projects for FY 2026-27
items.summary: Mountain View is planning its major infrastructure and public works spending for the upcoming fiscal year. This study session reveals what construction, facility upgrades, and community projects the city prioritizes over the next year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale proposes changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect housing rights and landlord-tenant relationships in Sunnyvale. Details on what specific protections are being added or modified were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: City releases Children and Youth Services Master Plan status update
items.summary: San José presented progress on its comprehensive Children and Youth Services Master Plan, along with annual reports on youth programs including the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. This signals the city's direction on youth services and community investment over coming years.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan moving forward
items.summary: The city provided a status update on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, indicating ongoing work to improve San José's library system. This plan will likely affect which libraries get renovated, expanded, or modified in the coming years.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic designation upgrade and 5,000 sq ft bonus
items.summary: A property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting to be elevated from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would grant an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area for rehabilitation work. This could affect the building's development potential and the character of downtown Palo Alto.
